 {"id":"bd-1nov","title":"Task: Add recovery config options","description":"## Purpose
Add configuration options for controlling session recovery behavior.

## Config Section
[recovery]
enabled = true                    # Master toggle
include_agent_mail = true         # Include recent messages
include_cm_memories = true        # Include procedural memories
include_beads_context = true      # Include BV task status
max_recovery_tokens = 2000        # Cap recovery context size
auto_inject_on_spawn = true       # Send automatically
stale_threshold_hours = 24        # Ignore context older than this

## Scope
- Add RecoveryConfig struct to internal/config/
- Parse from config.toml and environment variables
- Document options in example config
- Support per-session overrides via flags

## Acceptance Criteria
- All options configurable via config file
- Environment variable overrides work
- --no-recovery flag disables for single spawn
- Defaults are sensible (all enabled)

## Technical Notes

 {"id":"bd-1nqde","title":"Task: Parity Gate CI (kernel coverage + CLI/REST parity)","description":"## Scope\nAdd CI tests that prevent drift between CLI, robot mode, and REST/OpenAPI.\n\n## Implementation\n- Validate every kernel command has CLI + REST bindings.\n- Ensure every CLI command is registered in kernel.\n- Compare generated OpenAPI vs checked‑in spec (fail on diff).\n- CLI vs REST parity tests for representative commands.\n\n## Logging\n- Log missing bindings/commands with exact IDs and paths.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: kernel coverage tests.\n- Integration: parity harness comparing normalized CLI JSON vs REST JSON.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- CI fails on missing bindings or OpenAPI drift.\n- Parity tests catch output mismatches deterministically.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:37.823284186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757982074Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757424424Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ny8s","title":"Task: Write E2E tests for ensemble Robot API","description":"# Task: Robot API E2E Tests

End-to-end tests for ensemble robot commands with full JSON validation.

## Coverage
- --robot-ensemble
- --robot-ensemble-spawn
- --robot-ensemble-synthesize
- --robot-ensemble-modes (default core)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --tier=all (includes advanced)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --category=Formal (filter)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --limit/--offset (pagination)
- --robot-ensemble-presets (list)
- **advanced gating**: spawn with advanced mode without `--allow-advanced` (expect error), then with `--allow-advanced` (expect success)

## Logging Requirements
- Log raw JSON output
- Log parsed response structs
- Log session name + agent counts
- Log tier counts (core vs advanced)
- Log error_code + hint for gating failures

-{"id":"bd-1o1q1","title":"RCH: Add --robot-rch-status and --robot-rch-workers commands","description":"Add robot mode commands for RCH status and worker information.

## Command: --robot-rch-status

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-status
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "rch": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true,
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "workers": {
      "total": 3,
      "healthy": 2,
      "busy": 1
    },
    "session_stats": {
      "builds_total": 45,
      "builds_remote": 30,
      "builds_local": 15,
      "time_saved_seconds": 1200
    }
  }
}
```

## Command: --robot-rch-workers

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-workers [--worker=NAME]
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "workers": [
    {
      "name": "builder-1",
      "host": "192.168.1.100",
      "status": "healthy",
      "current_build": null,
      "builds_completed": 20,
      "cpu_percent": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "builder-2",
      "host": "192.168.1.101",
      "status": "busy",
      "current_build": "cargo build --release",
      "builds_completed": 25,
      "cpu_percent": 95
    }
  ]
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rch not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rch: cargo install rch"
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rch_status.go
- internal/robot/rch_workers.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rch-status` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--robot-rch-workers` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--worker=NAME` filter works for specific worker
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rch returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] Workers array includes all configured workers
- [ ] session_stats aggregates from current session
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rch_status_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] rch status parsing
- [ ] rch workers parsing
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rch
- [ ] Error handling for unreachable workers

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rch.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] Robot RCH Status Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot RCH E2E tests"

# Check if rch is available
if \! command -v rch &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rch not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Test 1: RCH status
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: RCH status"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-status)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".rch.version") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: RCH workers
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: RCH workers"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-workers)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq ".workers | type") == "\"array\"" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Missing rch fallback (if applicable)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Error handling"
# Mock test - verify error schema
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Error schema verified"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot RCH E2E tests passed"

 {"id":"bd-1o2r","title":"Fix double-width character handling (emoji, CJK)","description":"## Problem
Double-width characters (emoji, CJK, some Unicode) cause column misalignment because they take 2 terminal cells but count as 1 rune.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Already Handled Correctly

**The codebase uses `lipgloss.Width()` which internally uses `go-runewidth`:**

1. **table.go:205-210** - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// runeWidth returns the visual display width of a string.
// Uses lipgloss.Width() which properly handles ANSI escape codes and
// double-width characters (CJK, emoji) that occupy 2 terminal columns.
func runeWidth(s string) int {
    return lipgloss.Width(s)
}
```

2. **layout.go:93-128** - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// TruncateWidth trims a string to fit within maxWidth terminal columns,
// ...Unlike TruncateRunes, this uses lipgloss.Width()
// to properly account for double-width characters (CJK, emoji) and ANSI codes.
func TruncateWidth(s string, maxWidth int, suffix string) string {
    currentWidth := lipgloss.Width(s)
    ...
}
```

3. **badges.go:687-712** - truncation uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes and double-width runes.
```

### Non-Width Uses Are Correct

Found `utf8.RuneCountInString()` used in:
- **activity.go:81-82** - Counting characters for velocity metrics (chars/sec)
- **palette/model.go:1279** - Displaying "X chars" badge

Both are for CHARACTER COUNTING, not display width. Using `RuneCountInString` is correct there.

## Verification Tests Required

Add tests to verify double-width handling:

```go
func TestDoubleWidthCharacterWidth(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        input    string
        wantWidth int
    }{
        {"ASCII", "hello", 5},
        {"Emoji single", "😀", 2},
        {"Emoji sequence", "👨‍👩‍👧‍👦", 2},  // Family emoji (ZWJ sequence)
        {"CJK", "中文", 4},
        {"Mixed", "hi中文!", 7},
        {"Checkmark", "✓", 1},
        {"Warning", "⚠", 1},
        {"Full block", "█", 1},
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            got := lipgloss.Width(tc.input)
            if got != tc.wantWidth {
                t.Errorf("Width(%q) = %d, want %d", tc.input, got, tc.wantWidth)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestTableAlignmentWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Status", "Name", "Count")
    table.AddRow("✓", "Test 1", "10")
    table.AddRow("⚠", "Test 2 中文", "20")
    table.AddRow("✗", "Test 😀 3", "30")
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have same width (accounting for borders)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}

func TestTruncateWidthWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input    string
        maxWidth int
        want     string
    }{
        {"hello😀world", 8, "hello😀…"},  // emoji is 2 wide
        {"中文测试", 5, "中文…"},          // CJK truncation
        {"test中", 5, "test…"},           // Mixed truncation
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        got := layout.TruncateWidth(tc.input, tc.maxWidth, "…")
        gotWidth := lipgloss.Width(got)
        if gotWidth > tc.maxWidth {
            t.Errorf("TruncateWidth(%q, %d) = %q (width %d), exceeds max", 
                tc.input, tc.maxWidth, got, gotWidth)
        }
    }
}
```

## E2E Visual Test (VHS)
```tape

Set FontSize 14

 {"id":"bd-1xn3e","title":"Task: E2E Tests for Usage Limit Detection System","description":"## Task: E2E Tests for Usage Limit Detection System

### Why E2E Tests Are Critical

This system makes SAFETY decisions - whether to interrupt agent work or not. Unit tests verify logic, but E2E tests verify the entire chain works with REAL agents.

The user incident that triggered this EPIC was about interrupted work. We cannot ship this without verifying it actually protects working agents.

### Test Scenarios

#### 1. Working Agent Detection
- Spawn a Claude Code agent
- Send a prompt that triggers code generation
- While agent is actively writing code, call --robot-is-working
- VERIFY: is_working=true, recommendation=DO_NOT_INTERRUPT

#### 2. Idle Agent Detection
- Spawn a Codex agent
- Wait for prompt (no active task)
- Call --robot-is-working
- VERIFY: is_idle=true, recommendation=SAFE_TO_RESTART

#### 3. Context Percentage Parsing (Codex-specific)
- Spawn Codex agent
- Send multiple prompts to consume context
- Capture output showing "X% context left"
- Call --robot-is-working
- VERIFY: context_remaining matches the displayed percentage

#### 4. Rate Limited Detection
- Use a test account that hits rate limit (or mock)
- Call --robot-is-working
- VERIFY: is_rate_limited=true, recommendation=RATE_LIMITED_WAIT

#### 5. Smart Restart Safety - Working Agent
- Spawn Claude Code agent
- Send work prompt (agent becomes active)
- Attempt --robot-smart-restart (NO --force)
- VERIFY: action=SKIPPED, agent still running, work not interrupted

#### 6. Smart Restart Execution - Idle Agent
- Spawn Codex agent
- Wait for idle state
- Execute --robot-smart-restart
- VERIFY: action=RESTARTED, old agent exited, new agent launched

#### 7. caut Integration
- Verify caut client can query providers
- Verify agent type to provider mapping
- Verify graceful degradation when caut unavailable

#### 8. Health Score Accuracy
- Create agents in various states
- Call --robot-agent-health
- VERIFY: health scores match expected based on state

### Test Infrastructure

```go
package e2e

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "os/exec"
    "path/filepath"
    "strings"
    "testing"
    "time"
)

// TestLogger provides structured logging for E2E tests
type TestLogger struct {
    t         *testing.T
    logFile   *os.File
    startTime time.Time
    scenario  string
}

func NewTestLogger(t *testing.T, scenario string) *TestLogger {
    logDir := os.Getenv("E2E_LOG_DIR")
    if logDir == "" {
        logDir = "/tmp/ntm-e2e-logs"
    }
    os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0755)

    timestamp := time.Now().Format("20060102-150405")
    logPath := filepath.Join(logDir, fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s.log", scenario, timestamp))

    f, err := os.Create(logPath)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Failed to create log file: %v", err)
    }

    logger := &TestLogger{
        t:         t,
        logFile:   f,
        startTime: time.Now(),
        scenario:  scenario,
    }

    logger.Log("=== E2E Test Started: %s ===", scenario)
    logger.Log("Timestamp: %s", logger.startTime.Format(time.RFC3339))
    logger.Log("Log file: %s", logPath)

    return logger
}

func (l *TestLogger) Log(format string, args ...interface{}) {
    msg := fmt.Sprintf(format, args...)
    timestamp := time.Since(l.startTime).Round(time.Millisecond)
    line := fmt.Sprintf("[%s] %s\n", timestamp, msg)

    l.t.Log(msg)
    l.logFile.WriteString(line)
}

func (l *TestLogger) LogJSON(label string, v interface{}) {
    data, _ := json.MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ")
    l.Log("%s:\n%s", label, string(data))
}

func (l *TestLogger) Close() {
    duration := time.Since(l.startTime)
    l.Log("=== Test completed in %s ===", duration.Round(time.Millisecond))
    l.logFile.Close()
}

// TestSuite manages E2E test sessions
type TestSuite struct {
    t       *testing.T
    logger  *TestLogger
    session string
    panes   map[int]string // pane -> agent type
    cleanup []func()
}

func NewTestSuite(t *testing.T, scenario string) *TestSuite {
    logger := NewTestLogger(t, scenario)

    s := &TestSuite{
        t:       t,
        logger:  logger,
        session: fmt.Sprintf("e2e_%s_%d", scenario, time.Now().Unix()),
        panes:   make(map[int]string),
    }

    s.logger.Log("Creating test session: %s", s.session)
    return s
}

func (s *TestSuite) Setup() error {
    s.logger.Log("Setting up test environment")

    // Create tmux session
    cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", s.session, "-x", "200", "-y", "50")
    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
    }

    s.cleanup = append(s.cleanup, func() {
        s.logger.Log("Killing session %s", s.session)
        exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", s.session).Run()
    })

    s.logger.Log("Session created successfully")
    return nil
}

func (s *TestSuite) SpawnAgent(pane int, agentType string) error {
    s.logger.Log("Spawning %s agent in pane %d", agentType, pane)

    // Create new pane if needed
    if pane > 1 {
        cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "split-window", "-t", s.session, "-h")
        if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("create pane: %w", err)
        }
    }

    // Map agent type to command
    var agentCmd string
    switch agentType {
    case "cc":
        agentCmd = "cc"
    case "cod":
        agentCmd = "cod"
    case "gmi":
        agentCmd = "gmi"
    default:
        return fmt.Errorf("unknown agent type: %s", agentType)
    }

    // Launch agent
    sendCmd := fmt.Sprintf("tmux send-keys -t %s:%d %s Enter", s.session, pane, agentCmd)
    if err := exec.Command("bash", "-c", sendCmd).Run(); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("launch agent: %w", err)
    }

    s.panes[pane] = agentType
    s.logger.Log("Agent %s launched in pane %d", agentType, pane)

    // Wait for agent to initialize
    time.Sleep(6 * time.Second)

    return nil
}

func (s *TestSuite) SendPrompt(pane int, prompt string) error {
    s.logger.Log("Sending prompt to pane %d: %s", pane, truncate(prompt, 50))

    // Use ntm --robot-send
    cmd := exec.Command("ntm",
        fmt.Sprintf("--robot-send=%s", s.session),
        fmt.Sprintf("--panes=%d", pane),
        fmt.Sprintf("--msg=%s", prompt))

    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    if err != nil {
        s.logger.Log("Send failed: %v, output: %s", err, string(output))
        return err
    }

    s.logger.Log("Prompt sent successfully")
    return nil
}

func (s *TestSuite) CaptureOutput(pane int, lines int) (string, error) {
    s.logger.Log("Capturing %d lines from pane %d", lines, pane)

    cmd := exec.Command("ntm",
        fmt.Sprintf("--robot-tail=%s", s.session),
        fmt.Sprintf("--panes=%d", pane),
        fmt.Sprintf("--lines=%d", lines))

    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    s.logger.Log("Captured %d bytes", len(output))
    return string(output), nil
}

func (s *TestSuite) CallIsWorking(panes []int) (*IsWorkingResult, error) {
    s.logger.Log("Calling --robot-is-working for panes %v", panes)

    paneStr := strings.Trim(strings.Join(strings.Fields(fmt.Sprint(panes)), ","), "[]")

    cmd := exec.Command("ntm",
        fmt.Sprintf("--robot-is-working=%s", s.session),
        fmt.Sprintf("--panes=%s", paneStr))

    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        s.logger.Log("Command failed: %v", err)
        return nil, err
    }

    var result IsWorkingResult
    if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); err != nil {
        s.logger.Log("Parse failed: %v, output: %s", err, string(output))
        return nil, err
    }

    s.logger.LogJSON("IsWorking result", result)
    return &result, nil
}

func (s *TestSuite) CallSmartRestart(panes []int, force bool, dryRun bool) (*SmartRestartResult, error) {
    s.logger.Log("Calling --robot-smart-restart for panes %v (force=%v, dry-run=%v)",
        panes, force, dryRun)

    paneStr := strings.Trim(strings.Join(strings.Fields(fmt.Sprint(panes)), ","), "[]")

    args := []string{
        fmt.Sprintf("--robot-smart-restart=%s", s.session),
        fmt.Sprintf("--panes=%s", paneStr),
    }
    if force {
        args = append(args, "--force")
    }
    if dryRun {
        args = append(args, "--dry-run")
    }

    cmd := exec.Command("ntm", args...)
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        s.logger.Log("Command failed: %v", err)
        return nil, err
    }

    var result SmartRestartResult
    if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }

    s.logger.LogJSON("SmartRestart result", result)
    return &result, nil
}

func (s *TestSuite) WaitForState(pane int, check func(*PaneWorkStatus) bool, timeout time.Duration) bool {
    s.logger.Log("Waiting for state condition on pane %d (timeout: %v)", pane, timeout)

    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
    attempts := 0

    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
        attempts++
        result, err := s.CallIsWorking([]int{pane})
        if err == nil {
            status := result.Panes[fmt.Sprint(pane)]
            if check(&status) {
                s.logger.Log("State condition met after %d attempts", attempts)
                return true
            }
        }
        time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
    }

    s.logger.Log("State condition NOT met after %d attempts", attempts)
    return false
}

func (s *TestSuite) Teardown() {
    s.logger.Log("Running cleanup (%d items)", len(s.cleanup))

    // Run cleanup in reverse order
    for i := len(s.cleanup) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
        s.cleanup[i]()
    }

    s.logger.Close()
}

// Helper
func truncate(s string, n int) string {
    if len(s) <= n {
        return s
    }
    return s[:n] + "..."
}
```

### Test Implementation

```go
// e2e/usage_limit_test.go

func TestE2E_WorkingAgentDetection(t *testing.T) {
    if testing.Short() {
        t.Skip("Skipping E2E test in short mode")
    }

    suite := NewTestSuite(t, "working_agent")
    defer suite.Teardown()

    if err := suite.Setup(); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Setup failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Spawn Claude Code
    if err := suite.SpawnAgent(2, "cc"); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Spawn failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Send a prompt that triggers code generation
    prompt := "Write a Go function that calculates the Fibonacci sequence recursively. Include detailed comments."
    if err := suite.SendPrompt(2, prompt); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Send prompt failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Wait briefly for agent to start working
    time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)

    // Check state - agent should be working
    result, err := suite.CallIsWorking([]int{2})
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed: %v", err)
    }

    status := result.Panes["2"]

    // CRITICAL ASSERTION
    if !status.IsWorking {
        t.Errorf("Expected IsWorking=true while agent is generating code")
    }
    if status.Recommendation != "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT" {
        t.Errorf("Expected DO_NOT_INTERRUPT, got %s", status.Recommendation)
    }

    suite.logger.Log("SUCCESS: Working agent correctly detected")
}

func TestE2E_SmartRestart_ProtectsWorkingAgent(t *testing.T) {
    if testing.Short() {
        t.Skip("Skipping E2E test in short mode")
    }

    suite := NewTestSuite(t, "smart_restart_protection")
    defer suite.Teardown()

    if err := suite.Setup(); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Setup failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Spawn agent
    if err := suite.SpawnAgent(2, "cc"); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Spawn failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Make agent work on something substantial
    prompt := "Create a complete REST API server in Go with CRUD operations for a blog system. Include models, handlers, and routes."
    if err := suite.SendPrompt(2, prompt); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Send prompt failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Wait for work to begin
    time.Sleep(5 * time.Second)

    // Attempt restart WITHOUT force flag
    result, err := suite.CallSmartRestart([]int{2}, false, false)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("SmartRestart failed: %v", err)
    }

    action := result.Actions["2"]

    // CRITICAL: Agent must NOT be restarted
    if action.Action != "SKIPPED" {
        t.Fatalf("CRITICAL FAILURE: Working agent was not protected! Action=%s", action.Action)
    }

    // Verify agent is still running
    isWorkingResult, _ := suite.CallIsWorking([]int{2})
    if isWorkingResult != nil {
        status := isWorkingResult.Panes["2"]
        if status.AgentType != "cc" {
            t.Errorf("Agent type changed unexpectedly")
        }
    }

    suite.logger.Log("SUCCESS: Smart restart correctly protected working agent")
}

func TestE2E_CodexContextParsing(t *testing.T) {
    if testing.Short() {
        t.Skip("Skipping E2E test in short mode")
    }

    suite := NewTestSuite(t, "codex_context")
    defer suite.Teardown()

    if err := suite.Setup(); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Setup failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Spawn Codex
    if err := suite.SpawnAgent(2, "cod"); err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Spawn failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Wait for idle state with context percentage visible
    found := suite.WaitForState(2, func(s *PaneWorkStatus) bool {
        return s.IsIdle && s.ContextRemaining != nil
    }, 30*time.Second)

    if !found {
        t.Skip("Could not get Codex to idle state with context percentage")
    }

    result, _ := suite.CallIsWorking([]int{2})
    status := result.Panes["2"]

    if status.ContextRemaining == nil {
        t.Error("Expected ContextRemaining to be parsed from Codex output")
    } else {
        suite.logger.Log("Parsed context: %.1f%%", *status.ContextRemaining)

        // Context should be a reasonable percentage
        if *status.ContextRemaining < 0 || *status.ContextRemaining > 100 {
            t.Errorf("Context %.1f%% out of valid range", *status.ContextRemaining)
        }
    }

    suite.logger.Log("SUCCESS: Codex context percentage correctly parsed")
}

### CI Integration

```yaml
# .github/workflows/e2e-tests.yml
name: E2E Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  e2e:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.22'

      - name: Install tmux
        run: sudo apt-get install -y tmux

      - name: Install agent CLIs
        env:
          CLAUDE_TEST_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_TEST_TOKEN }}
          OPENAI_TEST_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_TEST_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          # Install cc, cod, gmi (mock or real depending on secrets)
          make install-test-agents

      - name: Run E2E tests
        env:
          E2E_LOG_DIR: /tmp/e2e-logs
          CAUT_MOCK_FILE: testdata/caut_mock.json
        run: |
          go test -v -timeout 20m ./e2e/... 2>&1 | tee e2e-output.log

      - name: Upload logs on failure
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: e2e-logs
          path: |
            /tmp/e2e-logs/
            e2e-output.log
```

 {"id":"bd-1xoe","title":"Fix content height management to prevent status bar duplication","description":"## Problem
The dashboard content height exceeds the terminal height, causing the status bar and help hints to be rendered multiple times as the user scrolls or the terminal content overflows.

## Root Cause Analysis (from code review)
The View() function in dashboard.go (line 1972) correctly uses a fresh strings.Builder each call. However:
1. Content height is not constrained to terminal height
2. No explicit height management for the main content area
3. Status bar and help bar are appended at the end without being positioned in a fixed footer region

## Actual Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:1972-2109 (View function)
- The View() builds content incrementally without checking if it exceeds m.height

## Fix Required
1. Calculate available content height: contentHeight := m.height - headerHeight - footerHeight
2. Constrain the main content area to contentHeight
3. Use lipgloss.JoinVertical with explicit heights for header/content/footer regions
4. Consider using a viewport for scrollable content areas

## Example Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) View() string {
    headerHeight := 6  // banner + session + divider
    footerHeight := 3  // ticker + divider + help
    contentHeight := m.height - headerHeight - footerHeight
    
    header := m.renderHeader()
    content := m.renderMainContent()
    footer := m.renderFooter()
    
    // Constrain content to available height
    contentStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Height(contentHeight).MaxHeight(contentHeight)
    
    return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
        header,
        contentStyle.Render(content),
        footer,
    )
}
\`\`\`

## Verification
- Dashboard fits within terminal without scrolling artifacts
- Resize terminal - content adapts without duplication
- Status bar and help bar appear exactly once at fixed positions

## Unit Tests Required
1. TestView_ContentFitsTerminal - View output height <= m.height
2. TestView_StatusBarAppearsOnce - count "Fleet:" occurrences == 1
3. TestView_HelpBarAppearsOnce - count "navigate" occurrences == 1
4. TestView_HeightCalculation - verify header+content+footer == total height

## Logging Requirements
- Log terminal dimensions on WindowSizeMsg
- Log calculated content heights
- Debug mode: log actual rendered content height","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:11:36.784501017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:51:05.418352445Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:51:05.418305687Z","close_reason":"Fixed by adding truncateToHeight helper and applying it in View() function to properly truncate content that exceeds available height","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xoe","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Unit tests for Agent Mail auto-registration functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### Agent Name Generation
```go
func TestAgentNameGeneration(t *testing.T) {
    // Test adjective+noun format
    name := generateAgentName()
    assert.Regexp(t, `^[A-Z][a-z]+[A-Z][a-z]+$`, name) // e.g., GreenCastle

    // Test uniqueness over multiple calls
    names := make(map[string]bool)
    for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
        n := generateAgentName()
        assert.False(t, names[n], "Duplicate name generated")
        names[n] = true
    }
}
```

### Pane to Agent Name Mapping
```go
func TestPaneToAgentNameMapping(t *testing.T) {
    mapper := NewPaneAgentMapper()

    // Register agents
    mapper.Register("cc_1", "GreenCastle")
    mapper.Register("cc_2", "BlueLake")

    // Lookup works
    name, ok := mapper.GetName("cc_1")
    assert.True(t, ok)
    assert.Equal(t, "GreenCastle", name)

    // Reverse lookup works
    pane, ok := mapper.GetPane("BlueLake")
    assert.True(t, ok)
    assert.Equal(t, "cc_2", pane)
}
```

### Registration Request Building
```go
func TestBuildRegistrationRequest(t *testing.T) {
    agent := AgentInfo{
        Type:    "cc",
        PaneID:  "cc_1",
        Workdir: "/data/project",
    }

    req := buildRegistrationRequest(agent)

    assert.Equal(t, "/data/project", req.ProjectKey)
    assert.Equal(t, "claude-code", req.Program)
    assert.NotEmpty(t, req.Name) // Auto-generated
}

### Error Handling
```go
func TestRegistrationErrorHandling(t *testing.T) {
    // Server unavailable - should not crash

    assert.Error(t, err)
    assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "Agent Mail server unavailable")

    // Invalid response - should log and continue
    // Registration failure should not block spawn
}

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.rano]
# Enable rano network monitoring
enabled = true

# Path to rano binary
binary_path = ""

# Polling interval in milliseconds
poll_interval_ms = 1000

# Track these providers (empty = all known)
providers = ["anthropic", "openai", "google"]

# Persist historical data
persist_history = true

# History retention days
history_days = 7
```

## Struct Definition

```go
type RanoConfig struct {
    Enabled        bool     `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath     string   `toml:"binary_path"`
    PollIntervalMs int      `toml:"poll_interval_ms"`
    Providers      []string `toml:"providers"`
    PersistHistory bool     `toml:"persist_history"`
    HistoryDays    int      `toml:"history_days"`
}

 {"id":"bd-1yty1","title":"CAAM: Implement agent environment update after account switch","description":"After CAAM switches accounts, update the agent's environment variables.

## Challenge

Agent processes are already running with old credentials in their environment.
We need to inject new credentials without restarting the agent.

## Approaches

### Option A: Environment File (Recommended)
Agent reads credentials from file that CAAM updates:
1. CAAM writes new token to ~/.config/caam/current/<provider>.json
2. Agent's hook or wrapper reads this file on each API call
3. No agent restart needed

### Option B: Signal + Re-read
1. NTM sends signal to agent process
2. Agent re-reads environment/config
3. Requires agent support for this pattern

### Option C: Session Restart (Fallback)
1. Save agent context/state
2. Kill agent process
3. Spawn new agent with new environment
4. Restore context (if possible)

## Implementation for Option A

```go
func (m *CAAMManager) UpdateAgentEnvironment(pane string, provider string) error {
    // Get new credentials from caam
    creds, err := m.GetCurrentCredentials(provider)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Write to agent's config location
    // (or rely on caam's built-in file updates)
    
    // For Claude Code: update ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env
    // For OpenAI: update OPENAI_API_KEY env
    // etc.
    
    return nil
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/caam/environment.go
- May need coordination with spawn code

## Testing
- Verify credentials file updated after switch
- Verify agent picks up new credentials
- Test concurrent switches in multi-agent scenario","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:51:20.597383033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:02:14.947980036Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:02:14.947937746Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yty1","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yty1","depends_on_id":"bd-26185","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yty1","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-1yty1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented agent environment update support:

1. Added `CAAMCredentials` struct for credential information (provider, account_id, api_key, token_path, env_var_name)
2. Added `ProviderEnvVars` map for provider -> environment variable mapping:
   - claude: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
   - openai: OPENAI_API_KEY
   - gemini: GOOGLE_API_KEY
3. Added helper functions:
   - `GetCredentialEnvVar(provider)`: Returns the env var name for a provider
   - `GetCredentialPath(provider)`: Returns ~/.config/caam/current/<provider>.json path
4. Added `GetCurrentCredentials(ctx, provider)` method that:
   - Calls `caam creds <provider> --json` if available
   - Falls back to `constructCredentials` helper for compatibility
5. Added comprehensive tests for all new functionality

This implements Option A (Environment File) from the bead spec - agents can read credentials from the caam-managed file path. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:02:05Z"}]}
